title: Decision on the Protocol Expanding the Scope of the Turkey - Azerbaijan Preferential Trade Agreement Published
bodyHtml:
The decision regarding the "Protocol Amending the Preferential Trade Agreement" between the Government of the Republic of Türkiye and the Government of the Republic of Azerbaijan has been published.

The Protocol, which envisages amendments to the PTA and was signed on April 29, 2023,  aims to increase the trade volume between the two countries in a balanced manner by introducing preferential arrangements for certain products in terms of goods trade.

In this context, during the process of expanding the product scope of the PTA in terms of goods trade, Türkiye has provided additional market access for a total of 15 tariff lines, including 8 industrial and 7 agricultural products. Azerbaijan, on the other hand, has provided additional market access for 15 tariff lines of industrial products.

Azerbaijan will grant additional market entry opportunities through reductions in customs duties ranging from 33% to 100% on certain fragrant substances, plastic bottles and flasks, wood products, goods made of paper and cardboard, copper wires, washing machines, some packaging machines, devices containing video recording or reproducing monitors and screens, and certain parts and accessories of motor vehicles.

Türkiye, in return, will provide additional market entry opportunities for Azerbaijan for products such as mulberries and quinces, certain beverages, some iron-steel products, packaging materials, and aluminum.

For the lists of products included in the agreement and the concessions granted under the amendment protocol, click here.
